Condition-Based Maintenance

The Smart Alternative to Time-Based Maintenance

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

What is Condition-Based Maintenance?

Condition-based maintenance (CBM) is a strategy where maintenance is scheduled based on the actual condition of equipment, not on predetermined time intervals. Instead of replacing or servicing equipment at fixed schedules, CBM uses monitoring data to determine when maintenance is truly needed.

Time-Based vs. Condition-Based

Traditional time-based maintenance assumes equipment deteriorates at a predictable rate. However, actual equipment degradation varies based on usage patterns, operating conditions, and inherent quality. This mismatch between assumed and actual degradation creates inefficiency:

  • Some equipment is serviced before problems develop (wasted maintenance)
  • Some equipment fails before scheduled service (unplanned downtime)

How CBM Works

CBM systems continuously monitor equipment health through sensors, electrical analysis, or other techniques. When monitoring data indicates a developing fault, maintenance is scheduled based on the severity and urgency of the condition.

Benefits

  • Lower maintenance costs through optimized scheduling
  • Reduced unplanned downtime
  • Extended equipment lifespan
  • Better resource planning and spare parts management
  • Improved safety through early problem detection

Getting Started

CBM implementation begins with identifying your most critical assets and selecting appropriate monitoring technologies for your fleet.
